truth.papers.A0.title: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
truth.papers.A0.paper_scope: Investigate co-precipitation behavior and mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel, including the effect of Cu addition on κ-carbide precipitation and mutual promotion between the two phases.

prediction.papers.A0.title: Co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel
prediction.papers.A0.paper_scope: Investigate the co-precipitation mechanism of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide particles in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel, clarify the mutual promotion effect of the two precipitates, their orientation relationship and coherence with austenite matrix.

truth.processes.proc_0cu_sol_age.description: Solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 h followed by aging treatment at 550°C for 3 h
truth.processes.proc_3cu_sol_age.description: Solution treatment at 1000°C for 1 h followed by aging treatment at 550°C for 3 h

prediction.processes.proc_0cu_sol_age.description: Solution treatment at 1000℃ for 1 h followed by aging treatment at 550℃ for 3 h
prediction.processes.proc_0cu_sol_age.processes_not: Applied to both 0Cu steel and 3Cu steel samples

prediction.processing_steps.A0.processing_steps_not: Applied to both 0Cu and 3Cu steel samples

truth.structures.sample_0cu.overall_structure: Austenitic matrix (γ) after solution and aging treatment; presumably no precipitates (only lattice parameter measured).
truth.structures.sample_0cu.microstructure_cou: Austenite matrix only; no precipitate characterization reported.
truth.structures.sample_3cu.overall_structure: Austenitic matrix (γ) with coherent precipitates of κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase.
truth.structures.sample_3cu.microstructure_cou: Two precipitate phases: κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase.
truth.structures.sample_3cu.microstructure_lis: mainly irregular oval (diameter ~10 nm) and a small amount of bars (length ~30 nm)

prediction.structures.sample_3cu.overall_structure: Austenitic matrix with co-precipitated Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide particles
prediction.structures.sample_3cu.microstructure_lis: almost no dislocations in Cu-rich phase

truth.interfaces.sample_3cu.interface_notes[0]: Lattice misfit values: γ/κ: 2.3%, γ/Cu-rich: 1.0%, κ/Cu-rich: 3.4%. All below 5%, confirming coherent interfaces.
truth.interfaces.sample_3cu.phase_evolution: κ-carbide precipitates first from austenitic matrix. Cu atoms are rejected from κ-carbide, enriching the surrounding matrix. When Cu concentration reaches critical level, Cu clusters form and precipitate as Cu-rich phase. Cu-rich phase nucleates in austenitic matrix near κ-carbide due to lower misfit with matrix (1.0%) compared to with κ-carbide (3.4%). The presence of κ-carbide promotes Cu-rich phase precipitation. Addition of Cu increases austenite lattice parameter, promoting κ-carbide precipitation.

prediction.interfaces.sample_3cu.phase_evolution: κ-carbide precipitates preferentially than Cu-rich phase, Cu atoms are excluded from κ-carbide and accumulate around κ-carbide, then Cu-rich phase precipitates when Cu concentration reaches critical value. κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase have cube-on-cube orientation relationship with austenitic matrix: [11\-0]γ//[11\-0]κ//[110]Cu, (11\-1)γ//(11\-1)κ//(11\-1)Cu.

truth.characterization_methods.sample_0cu.purp: Determine lattice parameter of austenitic matrix after solution treatment
truth.characterization_methods.sample_0cu.key_: Lattice parameter of 0Cu steel: a = 0.3687 nm
truth.characterization_methods.sample_3cu.tech: Energy dispersive spectrometer (EDS) mapping in TEM
truth.characterization_methods.sample_3cu.key_: κ-carbide regions are enriched in Fe, Mn, Al, and depleted in Cu. Cu-rich phase regions are enriched in Cu and depleted in Fe, Mn, Al. This indicates Cu partitioning to the matrix surrounding κ-carbide, leading to Cu-rich clustering and precipitation.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_3cu.key_: Cu atoms are excluded from κ-carbide precipitates, accumulate around κ-carbide to form Cu-rich phase, Fe/Mn/Al are excluded from Cu-rich phase
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_3.purpose: Measure lattice parameter of austenitic matrix
prediction.characterization_methods.idx_3.key_findi: Lattice parameter of solution treated 0Cu steel is 0.3687 nm, 3Cu steel is 0.3689 nm, FCC Cu-rich phase lattice parameter 0.3652 nm, κ-carbide lattice parameter 0.3775 nm

truth.computational_details.sample_3cu.analyti: Calculate lattice misfit between phases to assess coherency

Misfit between γ/κ: 2.3% (a0=0.3689 nm, a1=0.3775 nm); γ/Cu-rich: 1.0% (a0=0.3689 nm, a1=0.3652 nm); κ/Cu-rich: 3.4% (a0=0.3775 nm, a1=0.3652 nm). All < 5%, indicating coherent interfaces. Low misfit of Cu-rich with matrix promotes nucleation in matrix rather than on κ-carbide.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Calculate lattice misfit between two phases
δ = |a0 - a1| / a0, δ is lattice misfit, a0 and a1 are lattice parameters of the two phases
Calculated misfit between all three phases are less than 5%, confirming coherent relationship

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Cu addition to Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C steel increases the lattice parameter of austenitic matrix (from 0.3687 nm to 0.3689 nm), which promotes precipitation of κ-carbide.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The precipitation of κ-carbide expelled Cu atoms, locally increasing Cu concentration in surrounding matrix; upon reaching critical concentration, Cu clusters form and precipitate as Cu-rich phase.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: The critical lattice parameter of austenitic matrix for κ-carbide precipitation is approximately a = 0.3670 nm; below this, κ-carbide cannot precipitate. The calculation suggests a composition boundary formula exists for intragranular κ-carbide precipitation in Fe-Mn-Al-C steels.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The paper mentions that the yield strength of Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel is only 360-540 MPa (from reference), motivating Cu addition to improve strength, but no experimental mechanical testing results are reported in the current study.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Figure 1 shows SAED patterns indicating BCC structure for Cu-rich phase, but later text and HRTEM support FCC structure; this discrepancy is noted.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Figure 2 shows TEM and EDS images providing visual evidence of precipitate morphologies and elemental partitioning, but pure text mode cannot extract pixel-level details beyond captions and in-text descriptions.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Adding Cu to Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C austenitic low-density steel can increase the lattice parameter of austenitic matrix, promote the precipitation of κ-carbide, and κ-carbide can also promote the precipitation of Cu-rich phase.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The critical lattice parameter of austenitic matrix for κ-carbide precipitation is about 0.3670 nm, lattice parameter lower than this value will not form intragranular κ-carbide.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Al and C increase austenitic matrix lattice parameter, while Mn reduces austenitic matrix lattice parameter.
